---
title: "Installing"
description: "Install `check-datapackage` to use on the command-line and in Python projects."
order: 0
---

Before installing `check-datapackage`, you need
[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) installed. To check that it is
available, run:

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
uv --version
```

The command should print a version number. If you get an error instead,
install `uv` before continuing.

## Install globally

Installing `check-datapackage` globally lets you run it from any
directory on your machine. To install globally, run:

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
uv tool install check-datapackage
```

Once installed, you can run the program with `check-datapackage` (or
`cdp` for short). Alternatively, use `uvx` to run `check-datapackage`
without having to separately install it first---this always fetches the
latest version:

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
uvx check-datapackage
```

To enable shell auto-completions, run:

```{.bash filename="Terminal"}
check-datapackage --install-completion
```

::: callout-note
Completions only work for bash, fish, and zsh shells.
:::

## Install in a Python project

Installing `check-datapackage` in a project's virtual environment keeps
its dependencies contained within the project and not available
throughout the system.

We assume you've already created a Python project with a
`pyproject.toml` file. If not, review uv's
[projects](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/projects/) guide.

From your project's directory, run:

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
uv add check-datapackage
```

To verify the installation, run:

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
uv pip show check-datapackage
```

If successful, the output will show details about the installed package
and you're now ready to use `check-datapackage` in your project!
